hero.eyebrow: Mutual aid · Pet sitting · Home sitting
hero.lede: Have your home and pets looked after by a local caretaker / pet & home sitter during your absences. Or ask for, and return, a small favour near you, with or without pets.
hero.lede_italic: And behind every exchange, a meeting you wouldn't have looked for.

usages.title: In-home pet sitting and local mutual aid.
usages.lede: Home sitting on one side, mutual aid between locals on the other. You choose, one, the other, or both.

owner.title: Leave with peace of mind.
owner.text: Someone local watches over your home and your pets. You meet them before leaving. You choose.
owner.cta: I am looking for a caretaker / pet & home sitter →

sitter.title: Sit, and discover.
sitter.text: Live in homes, with pets, in corners you would never have explored otherwise. A sit is also a journey.

mutual.tag: Local mutual aid, independent of sitting
mutual.title: A small favour, an exchange.
mutual.text: Watering a vegetable garden, putting up a shelf, sharing a skill, visiting an isolated person. No sitting, no pet required, just locals helping each other.

urgency.title: A sudden departure?
urgency.text: Hospitalisation, last-minute travel, family emergency: a local caretaker / pet & home sitter can take over in a few hours.

meeting.eyebrow: What is never written in a listing
meeting.p1: What we rarely talk about is what happens around it. Getting to know the local grandmother and her story because you helped her pick her garden fruit. Exchanging with Gerardo while he gives you a small favour with DIY, and offering him a home-made meal in return.
meeting.p2: You don't need a pet for a meeting to begin. Asking for a small favour, offering one: it's already opening a door. Sitting a house is opening another, same gesture, two scales.
meeting.p3: We learn, we discover, we grow attached, without looking for it. Just by leaving space for what can happen.

how.title: How does it work?
how.step1_text: Your dates, your pets, your house. A few minutes are enough, your listing is online.
how.step2_text: Local caretaker / pet & home sitters apply. You read profiles, reviews, you chat. Then you meet, a coffee, a visit, before deciding.
how.step3_text: Once the sitting agreement is signed, your sitter moves in. You receive news. You return home, often with a new relationship.
how.cta_secondary: Or start with a small favour →
how.footnote: Registration in 2 minutes · No credit card required

aid.eyebrow: For everyone · No subscription · No money
aid.title: Dare to ask. Dare to propose.
aid.p1: Before, there was someone local who would come by to water the garden, who looked after the dog, who came to fix a shutter. Someone to whom we offered a piece of cake or a small favour in return.
aid.p2: This habit hasn't disappeared, we simply no longer dare to ask the first question. Guardiens is the pretext to ask it again, and along the way, live experiences that no organised trip could offer you.
aid.need_text: Your vegetable garden during holidays. Your chickens this weekend. Walking your dog after an operation. Asking for it isn't admitting a weakness, it's trusting.
aid.need_footer: No money, no subscription. That's the bet.
aid.offer_title: « I can give a little of my time »
aid.offer_text: You know how to prune roses. You love dogs. You have two hours on Saturday morning. Propose, and live what you would never have experienced without this meeting.
aid.offer_footer: That is how a local fabric reweaves itself.
aid.seen_this_week: A few exchanges seen on Guardiens this week
aid.quote: Village life hasn't disappeared. It was simply waiting for someone to dare the first question.

trust.title: Choose with confidence, one mile away or a thousand.
trust.lede: Four benchmarks for leaving peacefully: free for owners, a meeting before every sit, verified profiles, and a perimeter you set yourself.
trust.p1_text: Listing, applications, messaging: everything is free. No credit card requested.
trust.p2_text: A coffee, a visit of the accommodation, a tour of the instructions. Trust isn't guessed: it is built, face to face.
trust.p3_text: Identity document checked within 24h, reviews published by previous owners, history of completed sits. Everything is transparent.
trust.p4_title: From your street to the other side of France
trust.p4_text: Hyper-local for a quick fix, expanded for a change of scenery: you decide how far you want to go. Every sit becomes an opening door.

testimonials.title: They dared. Here is what they kept.
testimonials.source: Testimonials collected from Founder programme members (January – May 2026). Real first names and cities, stories anonymised at their request.

story.title: It all started with a visa.
story.p1: The habit of opening up to locals has been lost. For lack of a pretext.
story.p2: Elisa, my partner and co-founder of Guardiens, arrived from Argentina with a visa that didn't allow her to work. She looked after pets. She would come home with stories: people opening their doors, their lives, freely. Strangers who ended up inviting us for Christmas.
story.p3: Elisa and I looked after 37 houses in five years, in villages, cities, and hamlets.
story.quote: We never looked after houses. We were invited into lives.
story.p4: That's what the village used to do naturally.
story.p5: Guardiens is the pretext that was missing. On one side, small favours of mutual aid between local people: a vegetable garden watered in exchange for a meal, one skill for another, a small favour returned in kind. On the other, independently, house-sitting: your keys entrusted to someone you've looked in the eye.
story.p6: You will leave with peace of mind. You will return with a story. We didn't expect it to matter so much.
story.p7: That's why we built Guardiens, so you could live this too.

cities.title: House-sitting near you.
cities.lede: Concrete guides to prepare your sit, and local hubs for the most active cities, Lyon, Annecy, Grenoble, and all over France.
cities.cities_footer: Whatever your department, you will find a sitter nearby.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: House-sitting is a service exchange: a caretaker / pet & home sitter lives free of charge in your house during your absence and takes care of your pets. The exchange involves no financial transaction between the two parties.
faq.q2: Do I have to pay to register as an owner?
faq.a2: Registration, publishing listings, and exchanges with sitters require no subscription for owners. No credit card requested. Caretaker / pet & home sitters benefit from subscription-free access until July 14, 2026, then the subscription is €6.99/month. Mutual aid between locals remains subscription-free for everyone.
faq.q3: How can I find a pet sitter near me?
faq.a3: Register on Guardiens, publish your sitting listing with dates and your pets, and receive applications from caretaker / pet & home sitters who live near you. You choose after a meeting.
faq.q4: Who are the sitters on Guardiens?
faq.a4: Local people, verified by our team. Every profile undergoes an identity check (ID + selfie) processed within 24 hours. You can also see their reviews published by previous owners.
faq.q5: How does a sit take place on Guardiens?
faq.a5: You publish your listing, local caretaker / pet & home sitters apply, you choose after a meeting, then your sitter moves in. An optional sitting agreement frames the commitments of each party during the sit.
faq.q6: Is Guardiens available everywhere in France?
faq.a6: Yes. The Guardiens network extends to all regions of France, from the Basque Country to Brittany, through the Alps and the North. You will find a sitter near you regardless of your department.

final.title: Your next story begins here.
final.lede: You will leave with peace of mind. You will return with something else, a discovered corner, a meeting, someone you would never have crossed paths with.
final.footnote: Registration in 2 minutes, no credit card required.

mutual.title: Mutual aid, independent of sitting
mutual.p1: Small favours don't need animals to exist. Nadine is looking for a small favour gathering her vegetables. Jacques spends an hour in her garden and leaves with a homemade dish. No sitting, no credit card, no commission, just an exchange between locals.
mutual.p2: Cooking classes, watering plants, furniture assembly, loaning a drill, shared trip to the station: everything goes through mutual aid, without subscription.

emergency_page.meta_title: Emergency Pet Sitter, Care in less than 24h | Guardiens
emergency_page.meta_description: Need an emergency pet sitter? Activate the Guardiens alert and find a verified sitter near you in less than 24 hours.
emergency_page.soon_title: Coming soon. The emergency sitter network will activate as soon as we have enough verified and proven profiles.
emergency_page.soon_subtitle: Our platform is young. We take the time to designate emergency sitters on strict criteria to guarantee reliability.
emergency_page.hero_lead: Your local safety net. An unexpected event, a last-minute cancellation, a sudden departure? Trained local sitters, verified, mobilizable in less than 24h.
emergency_page.hero_tagline: The strength of proximity: a solution in hours, not days.
emergency_page.step1_title: An owner has an urgent need
emergency_page.step1_desc: Last-minute cancellation, family emergency, work trip… life doesn't always warn you.
emergency_page.step2_desc: Automatic notification sent to available emergency sitters within a 35 km radius.
emergency_page.step3_title: Care is organized in a few hours
emergency_page.step3_desc: Quick exchange, confirmation, off we go. Pets are in good hands.
emergency_page.stories_lead: Emergencies don't always look the way you imagine. Six real situations on Guardiens, all solved in less than 24 hours.
emergency_page.story1_text: Scheduled hospitalization moved up at the last minute. Two cats at home, no one around. She activated the alert Tuesday evening. A verified, rated emergency sitter replied within two hours. Meeting the next morning before departure. On return, cats still there, fridge too.
emergency_page.story2_text: Five-year-old Australian Shepherd, magnificent but reactive with strangers. Pierre had to leave for five days on a work trip. No question of a kennel. He activated the alert. An emergency sitter trained for strong-tempered dogs replied the same evening. First meeting outside, neutral ground. Pierre left reassured.
emergency_page.story3_text: Sudden death of a relative in the Paris region. Camille had to leave for four days, her 14-year-old cat can no longer handle moving. Alert sent in the morning, a local emergency sitter replied before noon. The cat didn't budge. Neither did Camille, in a way.
emergency_page.story4_text: Diabetic dog, two daily injections at fixed times. No one in her circle knew how. Sophie had to be away three days. She activated the alert specifying the care skill. An emergency sitter with vet experience replied that day. Injection demo the morning of departure. Everything went as planned.
emergency_page.story5_text: Unexpected consulting mission abroad, departure in 36 hours. Two cats at home, no family in town. Thomas activated the alert Thursday evening. Friday morning, an emergency sitter came to meet the cats and get the keys. Six days away, zero stress on the cats' side.
emergency_page.story6_text: Rushed move, the previous owner brought the walk-through forward by a week. Two stressed cats, an apartment under works, no time. The alert went out at 9am, an emergency sitter replied at 11am. The cats finished the week peacefully at the sitter's place.
emergency_page.owner_title: The peace of mind of trusted locals
emergency_page.owner_card1_desc: No need to wait days. Emergency sitters reply quickly.
emergency_page.owner_card2_desc: 5+ completed stays, rating 4.7+, 0 cancellations, verified identity.
emergency_page.owner_card3_desc: Automatically notified within a 35 km radius, they are locals, not strangers.
emergency_page.owner_cta: Sign up and be notified of the launch
emergency_page.sitter_subtitle: When you meet the conditions, the invitation will appear on your dashboard. You choose.
emergency_page.adv4: 1 month of subscription offered per intervention
emergency_page.engagement_title: A commitment, not just a badge
emergency_page.engagement_p1: Being an emergency sitter is a commitment. If you decline an urgent request, no worries, it happens. But if you decline more than once, the status is removed and you cannot reactivate it for 6 months. Owners count on emergency sitter availability.
emergency_page.engagement_p2: Cancellations are reassessed every 6 months: only those from the last 6 months count for eligibility.
emergency_page.cities_lead: The emergency network relies on local sitters across France. Here are the cities where emergency sitters are most active.
emergency_page.further1_desc: For one-off helping hands (without urgency) between locals.
emergency_page.further2_desc: Vets, parks, dog-friendly cafes and good spots near you.
emergency_page.further3_desc: The complete guide to join the Guardiens emergency sitter network.
emergency_page.faq1_q: How long to be contacted in an emergency?
emergency_page.faq1_a: On Guardiens, emergency alerts are prioritized: eligible emergency sitters in your radius are notified instantly (SMS, email, push). On average, you get a response in less than 4 hours, and a meeting within 24 hours.
emergency_page.faq2_q: Does it work at night or on weekends?
emergency_page.faq2_a: Yes. Emergency alerts are active 24/7. At night and on weekends, notifications go first to sitters who indicated extended availability. Response times can be a bit longer in atypical slots (up to 6 hours), but the service remains active.
emergency_page.faq3_q: My dog is reactive or anxious, is it still possible?
emergency_page.faq3_a: Yes, it's even a typical emergency case. When you activate the alert, you specify your pet's profile (reactive, anxious, special care, etc.). Only emergency sitters with the required experience and skill are notified. No sitter takes a stay without meeting the pet first, that's the rule.
emergency_page.faq4_q: How much does an emergency stay cost?
emergency_page.faq4_a: For owners, signing up and using Guardiens (including the emergency alert) are free. No surcharge applies for an emergency stay compared to a classic one. The house-sitting logic remains: no money flows between you and the sitter.
emergency_page.faq5_q: What's the difference with a classic stay?
emergency_page.faq5_a: A classic stay is prepared in advance: you post your listing, get several applications, choose. An emergency stay is immediate: an eligible sitter (5 stays minimum, rating 4.7+, 0 cancellations, verified ID) replies quickly, you meet them the same day or the next, the stay starts within 24h.
emergency_page.faq6_q: What if I'm hospitalized, how does it work?
emergency_page.faq6_a: It's one of the most frequent cases. You (or a relative) activate the alert directly from your dashboard or by phone. If you cannot, a relative can do it for you with your consent. The emergency sitter takes over: house, pets, mail if needed. You take everything back on your return.
emergency_page.faq7_q: How do I become an emergency sitter on Guardiens?
emergency_page.faq7_a: Four conditions: have completed at least 5 stays on Guardiens, have an average rating above 4.7/5, no cancellations, and have a verified identity. Once these criteria are met, you can activate the status from your dashboard.
emergency_page.faq8_q: What exactly do I commit to as an emergency sitter?
emergency_page.faq8_a: To respond quickly to the alerts you receive (push, SMS, email). You are not obliged to accept each request: you can decline if you're unavailable. But you commit to responding, not ignoring.
emergency_page.faq9_q: What happens if I decline an emergency request?
emergency_page.faq9_a: The first decline has no consequence, it's normal not to always be available. However, a second consecutive decline causes loss of status for 6 months. It's our guarantee to owners: an emergency sitter is truly available.
emergency_page.faq10_q: What do I gain by being an emergency sitter?
emergency_page.faq10_a: Priority visibility in search results, early access to interesting stays, distinctive badge on your profile, and one month of subscription offered per emergency intervention. Above all: you provide a real service to locals who truly need it.
emergency_page.faq11_q: How am I notified of an emergency?
emergency_page.faq11_a: SMS + email + push notification (if enabled). You configure your preferences from the dashboard: intervention radius (5 to 200 km), availability time slots, accepted pet types. Only alerts matching your preferences reach you.
emergency_page.faq12_q: Can I lose my emergency sitter status?
emergency_page.faq12_a: Yes, in several cases: rating drops below 4.7 (status paused, recoverable), cancellation of an accepted stay (immediate loss, reactivable after 5 new incident-free stays), second consecutive emergency decline (6-month suspension), expired subscription (status paused).

small_missions_page.meta_description: Helping hands, exchanges, skills. Among locals who choose each other.
small_missions_page.skill_prompt: Dare to say what you can do. Even a small talent can change someone's week.
small_missions_page.empty_offer_title: No offers within {{km}} km of you.
small_missions_page.empty_need_title: No requests within {{km}} km of you.
small_missions_page.empty_offer_text_no_oz: Be the first to offer your help. Post what you can do and locals will come to you.
small_missions_page.empty_need_text_no_oz: Be the first to post your need. Today's request is seen by locals tomorrow, and often a meeting that changes the week.
small_missions_page.publish_offer_cta: I dare, I publish my offer
small_missions_page.publish_need_cta: I dare, I publish my request
small_missions_page.oz_subtitle: Beyond your current area ({{km}} km). Expand to see them all.
small_missions_page.empty_helpers_offer_title: You'd be the first person available here.
small_missions_page.empty_helpers_need_title: No one has yet declared themselves available near you.
small_missions_page.empty_helpers_offer_text: Toggle your availability above: your presence inspires others to dare.
small_missions_page.empty_helpers_need_text: Expand the radius, or post your request: locals receive an alert and often respond within the day.

missions_hero.title: Small missions near you
missions_hero.subtitle: Ask for a helping hand or offer yours, among local folks, no money involved.
missions_hero.cta_publish: I'll do it, post my request
missions_hero.cta_propose: or make myself visible as a helper →

mission_card.add_bio_cta: Add a short bio to reassure sitters

offer_dialog.title: I have time to offer
offer_dialog.description: Indicate the areas where you can help, add your specific skills and describe what you offer.
offer_dialog.saved_subtitle: Local members can now find you.
offer_dialog.free_text_placeholder: E.g., I can water plants, walk a dog, help with small DIY jobs…

create_mission_page.meta_title_offer: I have time to give | Guardiens
create_mission_page.meta_title_need: I'm asking for a hand | Guardiens
create_mission_page.meta_description: Post a request or an offer of mutual aid. Helping hands between locals, no money involved.
create_mission_page.encouragement_title: Hesitating? That's normal. No one will judge you.
create_mission_page.encouragement_offer: Describe what you can do, even modestly. Someone nearby might need exactly that.
create_mission_page.encouragement_need: Describe your need, even a small one. You're not bothering anyone, you're giving someone a chance to help.
create_mission_page.note_text: small missions are one-off helping hands, with no overnight stay at the person's home.
create_mission_page.templates_subtitle: Click a template to pre-fill the form, you can adjust everything afterwards.
create_mission_page.title_ph_offer: E.g. I can walk a dog on weekends
create_mission_page.title_ph_need: E.g. Walk my dog 3 times this week
create_mission_page.desc_ph_offer: Describe what you can do. Be concrete: when you're available, how often, what people need to know.
create_mission_page.desc_ph_need: Describe what you need. Be precise: when, how long, what people need to know.
create_mission_page.exchange_ph_offer: E.g. A shared meal, vegetables, or "to be discussed together"
create_mission_page.exchange_ph_need: E.g. A good meal, vegetables from the garden, a fruit basket...
create_mission_page.exchange_error_euros: Here we exchange helping hands and good meals, not euros.
